questions.prompt: Why pin action versions (e.g., `actions/checkout@v4`) instead of `@main`?

choices.text: `@main` always pulls the latest commit on the action's main branch — breaking changes can land anytime and silently break your workflows. Pinned versions (`@v4`, `@v4.1.2`, or a commit SHA) give reproducibility: your workflow runs the same way today as it did last month. Use major version tags (`@v4`) for most cases — get patch and security updates automatically while staying within a stable API. Pin to exact SHA for high-security workflows where you want zero unverified changes. Dependabot can keep pinned versions up to date with notifications when new major versions release.

questions.explanation: @main can break anytime. Pin to major version (or SHA) for reproducibility and safety.

questions.prompt: CI builds taking 4 minutes; most of it is reinstalling npm packages. Fix?

choices.text: Cache npm installs. Easiest: use `actions/setup-node@v4` with `cache: 'npm'` — caches the npm cache directory keyed by package-lock.json hash. First build is normal; subsequent builds restore the cache and skip downloading unchanged deps. Typical: 4 minutes → 30 seconds for the install step. Same pattern works for pip (`cache: 'pip'`), yarn, pnpm, Go modules. For more control, use `actions/cache@v4` directly with custom keys. Builds get fast; team friction drops; CI minute costs drop.

questions.explanation: Use setup-node's built-in cache (or actions/cache). 4min → 30sec is typical.
